Please help 90FM continue to stay on the air by simply choosing Goodsearch as your search engine for Trivia or during the year. That's it.
Choose WWSP as your non-profit of choice!
How the Contest Works
Trivia teams of any size may bring the entire team or representatives to the 90FM studios on Reserve Street from 3 until 7 Monday through Thursday, and Friday from Noon until 6, the week of the contest to register. At that time, teams will be given one copy of the New Trivia Times. The New Trivia Times will also be available online at the 90fmtrivia.org website. The New Trivia Times contains information about the contest, the pictures, and other pertinent information, like weekend specials on food from area businesses who support Trivia, and other businesses who support 90FM and the contest.
Registration is $40.00 per team, and teams can be of any size.
Description of the Contest
Eight questions are asked over the 90FM airwaves each hour. Team contestants are given the length of time for two songs to play in which to respond with an answer. Each team is given one attempt to answer. If teams call more than once per question, they will receive zero points for that question. All teams that respond correctly are given an equal share of the 2000 points that a question is worth, with a minimum of 5 points and a maximum of 500 points. There are four hours during the contest when only four questions are asked. In two of these, there are only four questions asked. This allows us time to read all of the team standings. The other two are at midnight Friday and Saturday. During those hours, ten questions are asked and the songs are all four minutes or more.
Running Questions
The Running Questions are given at specific locations in Stevens Point at 7:00 A.M. both Saturday and Sunday mornings. Teams are asked to gather information within "running" distance of the meeting sight. Locations for the Running Questions are given in the Trivia Times.
Trivia Stone
The Trivia Stone is a rally, of sorts. During the contest, teams are instructed to follow a certain group of clues. These clues lead teams throughout the city and countryside, and at three times are instructed to get their Trivia Times booklet stamped. The stamps are then turned in at 6:30 Sunday evening for points. One stamp earns 50 points, two stamps 150 points, and three stamps 300 points. The first clue is given in the Trivia Times.
Music Questions
The music questions are snippets of 8 songs. There are three music questions, and each of the snippets is equivalent to one question. Question turn-in times are in your copy of The New Trivia Times. This is the booklet that you receive at the time of registration.
Awards
Trophies for first through tenth place are donated by Point Trophy.
*All Rules are subject to change, depending on OZ!!!
As in past years, the Internet is not a source of valid information, so if you have a question about an answer being correct, you need two sources, neither of which are the internet.
Welcome to the Trivia website. If you have any questions, please feel free to email me, I'll give you an answer.
The address is theoz@dwave.net.
The Trivia Forum has been inactive and has been closed down. Still want to put in your 2 cents? Go to Facebook and like us!
There is plenty going on there. Need a team to join, post that on Facebook as well!
